title: Citrusy and Snappy Argentinian Empanada Patties
description: A twist on traditional empanadas, these Argentinian empanada patties are citrusy, snappy, and crowd-pleasing, featuring raisins and a hint of ferment, all in a gluten-free-friendly package.

ingredients.name: raisins
ingredients.notes: soaked in 1/4 cup apple cider vinegar for at least 30 minutes

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine corn flour, raisins, water, egg, lime juice, salt, black pepper, cayenne pepper, and yeast ferment powder. Mix until a smooth dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes until it becomes pliable and smooth.
Divide the dough into 12 equal pieces and shape each piece into a ball.
Roll out each ball of dough into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat and brush with olive oil.
Cook the empanada patties for 2-3 minutes on each side, until they are golden brown and crispy.
Repeat the process with the remaining dough pieces.
Note: You can also bake the empanada patties in a preheated oven at 375°F (190°C) for 10-12 minutes, or until crispy and golden brown.
